name: UTX-1 H3K27 demethylase knockdown extended C. elegans lifespan
summary: UTX-1 supports a second histone-methylation axis for worm lifespan regulation.
statement: UTX-1, a H3K27 demethylase, regulated C. elegans lifespan through a germline-independent, insulin/DAF-16-dependent mechanism associated with H3K27me3 biology.
population_or_model: Caenorhabditis elegans H3K27me3, RNAi, daf-16, and lifespan assays
quantitative_note: The PubMed abstract reports lifespan regulation but does not provide one percentage effect size.
caveats: This is worm genetic/pathway evidence rather than a therapeutic intervention.
The pathway depends on insulin/DAF-16 biology and may overlap nutrient-sensing tracks.
H3K27 methylation biology is developmentally sensitive and not obviously safe to modulate chronically.
